name: filippo-skills
description: A collection of reusable skills for Claude Code

plugins.name: mode-router
plugins.description: Per-prompt router: classifies each request and invokes the caveman (terse output) or ponytail (minimal code) skill, exclusively. Force a mode or turn it off via control file. Bundles both as dependencies.

plugins.name: improve-codebase-architecture-bundle
plugins.description: Install improve-codebase-architecture together with the mattpocock skills it calls at runtime (grilling, codebase-design, domain-modeling). Bundles them as dependencies.

plugins.name: printing-press-bundle
plugins.description: Install printing-press with the sibling skills it invokes at runtime (printing-press-polish, printing-press-score, and the internal printing-press-output-review). Bundles them as dependencies.

plugins.name: implement-bundle
plugins.description: Install implement together with the skills it calls at runtime (tdd, code-review). Bundles them as dependencies.

plugins.name: triage-bundle
plugins.description: Install triage together with the skills it calls at runtime (grilling, domain-modeling). Bundles them as dependencies.

plugins.name: wayfinder-bundle
plugins.description: Install wayfinder together with the skills it calls at runtime (grilling, domain-modeling, prototype, research). Bundles them as dependencies.

plugins.name: grill-with-docs-bundle
plugins.description: Install grill-with-docs together with the skills it calls at runtime (grilling, domain-modeling). Bundles them as dependencies.

plugins.name: ask-matt
plugins.description: Ask which skill or flow fits your situation.

plugins.name: diagnosing-bugs
plugins.description: Diagnosis loop for hard bugs and performance regressions.

plugins.name: grill-with-docs
plugins.description: A relentless interview to sharpen a plan or design, which also creates docs (ADR's and glossary) as we go.

plugins.name: triage
plugins.description: Move issues and external PRs through a state machine of triage roles — categorise, verify, grill if needed, and write agent-ready briefs.

plugins.name: resolving-merge-conflicts
plugins.description: Use when you need to resolve an in-progress git merge/rebase conflict.

plugins.name: wayfinder
plugins.description: Plan a huge chunk of work — more than one agent session can hold — as a shared map of investigation tickets on your issue tracker, and resolve them one at a time until the way to the destination is clear.

plugins.name: improve-codebase-architecture
plugins.description: Scan a codebase for deepening opportunities, present them as a visual HTML report, then grill through whichever one you pick.

plugins.name: setup-matt-pocock-skills
plugins.description: Configure this repo for the engineering skills — set up its issue tracker, triage label vocabulary, and domain doc layout.

plugins.name: tdd
plugins.description: Test-driven development.

plugins.name: to-tickets
plugins.description: Break a plan, spec, or the current conversation into a set of tracer-bullet tickets, each declaring its blocking edges, published to the configured tracker — edges as text in a local file, or native blocking links on a real tracker.

plugins.name: to-spec
plugins.description: Turn the current conversation into a spec and publish it to the project issue tracker — no interview, just synthesis of what you've already discussed.

plugins.name: implement
plugins.description: Implement a piece of work based on a spec or set of tickets.

plugins.name: prototype
plugins.description: Build a throwaway prototype to answer a design question.

plugins.name: research
plugins.description: Investigate a question against high-trust primary sources and capture the findings as a Markdown file in the repo.

plugins.name: domain-modeling
plugins.description: Build and sharpen a project's domain model.

plugins.name: codebase-design
plugins.description: Shared vocabulary for designing deep modules.

plugins.name: code-review
plugins.description: Review the changes since a fixed point (commit, branch, tag, or merge-base) along two axes — Standards (does the code follow this repo's documented coding standards?) and Spec (does the code match what the originating issue/PRD asked for?).

plugins.name: grill-me
plugins.description: A relentless interview to sharpen a plan or design.

plugins.name: grilling
plugins.description: Grill the user relentlessly about a plan or design.

plugins.name: handoff
plugins.description: Compact the current conversation into a handoff document for another agent to pick up.

plugins.name: teach
plugins.description: Teach the user a new skill or concept, within this workspace.

plugins.name: writing-great-skills
plugins.description: Reference for writing and editing skills well — the vocabulary and principles that make a skill predictable.

plugins.name: brandkit
plugins.description: Premium brand-kit image generation skill for creating high-end brand-guidelines boards, logo systems, identity decks, and visual-world presentations.

plugins.name: brutalist-skill
plugins.description: Raw mechanical interfaces fusing Swiss typographic print with military terminal aesthetics.

plugins.name: gpt-tasteskill
plugins.description: Elite UX/UI & Advanced GSAP Motion Engineer.

plugins.name: image-to-code-skill
plugins.description: Elite website image-to-code skill for Codex.

plugins.name: imagegen-frontend-mobile
plugins.description: Elite mobile app image-generation skill for creating premium, app-native screen concepts and flows.

plugins.name: imagegen-frontend-web
plugins.description: Elite frontend image-direction skill for generating premium, conversion-aware website design references.

plugins.name: minimalist-skill
plugins.description: Clean editorial-style interfaces.

plugins.name: output-skill
plugins.description: Overrides default LLM truncation behavior.

plugins.name: redesign-skill
plugins.description: Upgrades existing websites and apps to premium quality.

plugins.name: soft-skill
plugins.description: Teaches the AI to design like a high-end agency.

plugins.name: stitch-skill
plugins.description: Semantic Design System Skill for Google Stitch.

plugins.name: taste-skill-v1
plugins.description: The original v1 taste-skill, preserved for projects depending on its exact behavior.

plugins.name: taste-skill
plugins.description: Anti-slop frontend skill for landing pages, portfolios, and redesigns.

plugins.name: caveman
plugins.description: Ultra-compressed communication mode.

plugins.name: ponytail
plugins.description: Forces the laziest solution that actually works, simplest, shortest, most minimal.

plugins.name: printing-press
plugins.description: Generate a ship-ready CLI for an API via a lean research → generate → build → shipcheck loop.

plugins.name: printing-press-polish
plugins.description: Polish a generated CLI to pass verification and become publish-ready: runs diagnostics (dogfood, verify, scorecard, go vet, gosec), auto-fixes issues, and reports the before/after delta.

plugins.name: printing-press-score
plugins.description: Score a generated CLI against the Steinberger bar, or compare two CLIs side-by-side.

plugins.name: printing-press-output-review
plugins.description: Internal sub-skill: agentic review of a printed CLI's sampled command output for plausibility issues rule-based checks can't catch. Invoked by printing-press and printing-press-polish; not for direct use.

plugins.name: api-scaffolding
plugins.description: REST and GraphQL API scaffolding: backend-architect, fastapi-pro, django-pro, and graphql-architect agents plus the fastapi-templates skill.

plugins.name: shell-scripting
plugins.description: Production-grade Bash scripting: bash-pro and posix-shell-pro agents plus the bash-defensive-patterns, bats-testing-patterns, and shellcheck-configuration skills.

plugins.name: nuxt
plugins.description: Nuxt full-stack Vue framework with SSR, auto-imports, and file-based routing.

plugins.name: fastify-best-practices
plugins.description: Guides development of Fastify Node.js backend servers and REST APIs using TypeScript or JavaScript.

plugins.name: react-expert
plugins.description: Use when building React 18+ applications in .jsx or .tsx files, Next.js App Router projects, or create-react-app setups.

plugins.name: wordpress-pro
plugins.description: Develops custom WordPress themes and plugins, creates and registers Gutenberg blocks and block patterns, configures WooCommerce stores, implements WordPress REST API endpoints, applies security hardening, and optimizes performance through caching and query tuning.

plugins.name: fastapi-expert
plugins.description: Use when building high-performance async Python APIs with FastAPI and Pydantic V2.

plugins.name: kubernetes-specialist
plugins.description: Use when deploying or managing Kubernetes workloads.

plugins.name: golang-pro
plugins.description: Implements concurrent Go patterns using goroutines and channels, designs and builds microservices with gRPC or REST, optimizes Go application performance with pprof, and enforces idiomatic Go with generics, interfaces, and robust error handling.

plugins.name: cli-developer
plugins.description: Use when building CLI tools, implementing argument parsing, or adding interactive prompts.

plugins.name: code-documenter
plugins.description: Generates, formats, and validates technical documentation — including docstrings, OpenAPI/Swagger specs, JSDoc annotations, doc portals, and user guides.

plugins.name: database-optimizer
plugins.description: Optimizes database queries and improves performance across PostgreSQL and MySQL systems.

plugins.name: playwright-expert
plugins.description: Use when writing E2E tests with Playwright, setting up test infrastructure, or debugging flaky browser tests.

plugins.name: sql-pro
plugins.description: Optimizes SQL queries, designs database schemas, and troubleshoots performance issues.

plugins.name: ui-ux-pro-max
plugins.description: UI/UX design intelligence for web and mobile.
